root_causes.title: Shell injection via ${{ inputs.* }} in run blocks
root_causes.description: Direct interpolation of GitHub Actions inputs in bash scripts creates shell injection vulnerabilities.
root_causes.solution_summary: Move inputs to the env context and reference them as environment variables.

root_causes.title: Duplicated constants and domain-leakage in _optional_surface_common
root_causes.description: Constants drift and reporting logic leaked into common surface plumbing.
root_causes.solution_summary: Extract reporting IO, import LOCK_PATH from contracts, add keep-in-sync guards.

root_causes.title: KB scripting read/write bugs, perf, CI workflows, and documentation
root_causes.description: A group of write, performance, CI issues, and docs drift. Grouped because their test files heavily overlap and they share core dependencies. Includes ADR-011 violations, synthetic SHAs, missing update locks, missing symlink checks, no-op flags, batch mode support, test coverage, and docs drift.
root_causes.solution_summary: Fix all scripting issues, harden symlinks, add lock, fix perf, fix workflows, and update docs.

tasks.title: Fix shell injection in fleet workflows
tasks.prompt:
Fix shell injection via `${{ inputs.base_branch }}` in fleet workflows.

Files to modify:
- `.github/workflows/fleet-dispatch.yml`
- `.github/workflows/fleet-merge.yml`

Root Cause Explanation:
Direct interpolation of `${{ inputs.base_branch }}` in `run:` blocks allows shell injection.
For example, in `.github/workflows/fleet-dispatch.yml`:
```yaml
          BASE="${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}"
```
If an attacker passes a branch name like `main"; echo "hacked`, the shell executes the injected command.

Implementation & Integration:
In `fleet-dispatch.yml`, map inputs in the `env` context and replace usages:
```diff
       - name: Dispatch task sessions
         working-directory: scripts/fleet
         env:
           JULES_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.JULES_API_KEY }}
           GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
-          FLEET_BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}
+          FLEET_BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}
+          INPUT_BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
-          git pull --ff-only origin "${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}"
+          git pull --ff-only origin "${INPUT_BASE_BRANCH}"
           bun fleet-dispatch.ts
```

In `fleet-merge.yml`:
```diff
       - name: Find and merge fleet PRs sequentially
+        env:
+          INPUT_BASE_BRANCH: ${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}
         run: |
           set -euo pipefail
 
-          BASE_BRANCH="${{ inputs.base_branch || 'main' }}"
+          BASE_BRANCH="${INPUT_BASE_BRANCH}"
```
Make sure to do this for all instances. Also use `$PR_NUMBER` instead of `${{ steps.wait_pr.outputs.pr_number }}`.

Test scenarios:
- Normal branch name `main` works.
- Branch name with shell characters does not execute code.

Acceptance criteria:
- No `${{ inputs.* }}` inside `run:` blocks in modified files.

You may ONLY modify the files listed above. If a test file outside your boundary fails, you must make your source changes backward-compatible so the existing test passes unmodified. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

tasks.title: Refactor optional surface common and reporting
tasks.prompt:
Refactor `_optional_surface_common.py` to fix duplication and extract reporting IO.

Root Cause Explanation:
`_optional_surface_common.py` has accumulated reporting logic and duplicated constants.
```python
# scripts/_optional_surface_common.py:440
def validate_report_artifact(artifact: dict, report_type: str) -> None:
```
It should import it from contracts. And qmd_preflight duplicates STATUS_PASS.

Implementation & Integration:
1. Move `validate_report_artifact`, `write_report_artifact`, `REPORT_ARTIFACT_WRITE_ROOT`, `_REPORT_TYPE_FINDINGS_KEYS`, `_REPORT_TYPE_SUMMARY_KEYS`, and `_REPORT_ENVELOPE_REQUIRED` to `scripts/reporting/_artifact.py`. Add `__all__` listing the 3 public names.
```python
# scripts/reporting/_artifact.py
__all__ = ["validate_report_artifact", "write_report_artifact", "REPORT_ARTIFACT_WRITE_ROOT"]
```
2. In `scripts/reporting/content_quality_report.py` and `scripts/reporting/quality_runtime.py`, import `write_report_artifact` from `scripts.reporting._artifact`.
```diff
-from scripts._optional_surface_common import write_report_artifact
+from scripts.reporting._artifact import write_report_artifact
```
3. In `scripts/kb/qmd_preflight.py`, add keep-in-sync guards:
```diff
-STATUS_PASS = "pass"
+STATUS_PASS = "pass"  # keep in sync with scripts/_optional_surface_common.STATUS_PASS
```
4. In `scripts/_optional_surface_common.py`, replace `LOCK_PATH = "wiki/.kb_write.lock"` with `from scripts.kb.contracts import WRITE_LOCK_PATH as LOCK_PATH`. Update comments.

Test scenarios:
- Report generation creates correct JSON structure and file path
- Tests continue to pass

Acceptance criteria:
- All usages of moved symbols still resolve.
- Tests pass.

You may ONLY modify the files listed above. If a test file outside your boundary fails, you must make your source changes backward-compatible so the existing test passes unmodified. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

tasks.title: Fix KB write, indexing, persist, perf, CI, and doc issues
tasks.prompt:
Fix a cluster of highly-coupled KB script, documentation, and CI issues including locking, symlink guards, performance, cross boundary imports, and workflow configs.

Root Cause Explanation:
- `ingest.py` imports private symbols from its sibling `ingest_render.py` violating ADR-011.
- `ingest.py` uses a synthetic SHA `00000...` and processes one by one via CLI loop.
- `update_index.py` --write mode writes to the index without a lock.
- `lint_wiki.py` has duplicate filesystem parses `list(wiki_root.rglob("*.md"))` and redundant stat ops.
- `write_utils.py` lacks symlink bounds checks when writing files.
- `persist_query.py` unconditionally rebuilds index and has unused flags.
- `raw/inbox/SPEC.md` says ingest generates entities.
- `docs/architecture.md` spec says ingest generated entities and lacks Fleet section.
- Missing test coverage gate, lock-file deletion bypassing governance, missing authoritative approval, duplicate parsing, inefficient source list handoff. Missing test coverage for workflow SHA pinning. Missing gitignore patterns.

Implementation & Integration:
1. In `scripts/kb/ingest_render.py`, rename `_PROVISIONAL_GIT_SHA`, `_build_source_ref`, `_build_provisional_source_provenance`, `_render_source_page`, and `_escape_quotes` to remove the leading underscore. Add an `__all__` list. Update callers in `ingest.py`.
2. In `scripts/kb/ingest.py`, read GITHUB_SHA from env or fallback to subprocess `git rev-parse HEAD` for `PROVISIONAL_GIT_SHA`. Modify the CLI to accept a manifest file for batching multiple files. Loop over the files before calling `_write_index_if_changed`.
3. In `scripts/kb/update_index.py`, import `write_utils` and use `with write_utils.exclusive_write_lock(wiki_root.parent):` around index write.
4. In `scripts/kb/lint_wiki.py` and `scripts/kb/update_index.py`, reduce repeated path existence/stat operations. Cache the `path.exists()` calls for links.
5. In `scripts/kb/write_utils.py`, add logic to ensure resolved paths are inside repo boundaries using `Path.resolve()` and `Path.is_relative_to()`. Raise `OSError` if outside. Use this in `write_text_capturing_previous_safe`.
```python
resolved = path.resolve()
if not resolved.is_relative_to(repo_root):
    raise OSError("Escape attempted")
```
6. In `scripts/kb/persist_query.py`, remove `--result-json` and contradiction gate CLI arguments. Add logic to gate index regeneration on `analysis_changed` state.
7. Update `raw/inbox/SPEC.md` and `docs/architecture.md` removing entity generation claims. Add 'Fleet orchestration' section to `docs/architecture.md`.
8. Update corresponding tests in the `test_files` boundary to cover batching, real SHAs, write bounds against `/etc/passwd` via symlink, and persist_query flag removals.
9. In `ci-2-analyst-diagnostics.yml`, enforce coverage gate using pytest-cov `--cov=scripts/kb --cov-fail-under=90` and remove duplicate workflow YAML parsing step (Ruby script).
10. In `ci-3-pr-producer.yml`, enforce authoritative approval for manual dispatch (`environment: protected-pr-write`). Remove the `rm -f wiki/.kb_write.lock` step. Write source list to a file `ingest_manifest.txt` instead of using `GITHUB_OUTPUT` multiline string. Support the batch mode argument in `ingest.py` call.
11. Add `.env.local`, `.env.*.local`, `.claude/` to `.gitignore`.
12. Add tests in `tests/validation/test_workflow_actions.py` asserting workflow YAML uses SHA pins for actions `uses: actions/checkout@[a-f0-9]{40}`. Also add coverage tests for verification matrix gaps.

Test scenarios:
- Validate that the new SHA is a valid 40 char string
- Pass multiple files in a manifest and ensure only 1 index rebuild occurs.
- Run concurrent index updates to verify locking prevents corruption
- A write attempt to `../../outside_repo.txt` via symlink raises OSError.
- Persist query skips index rebuild if output matches state.

Acceptance criteria:
- No symlink escapes are possible.
- Real SHA is used.
- Batching works.
- Locks are enforced.
- Tests pass.
- Workflows are properly formatted and tested.
- Docs updated.

You may ONLY modify the files listed above. If a test file outside your boundary fails, you must make your source changes backward-compatible so the existing test passes unmodified. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

unaddressable.reason: Requires explicit baseline metrics discussion and CI capacity planning.
